title: Mellow Peppery French Chicken Coq Au Vin
description: A classic French dish with a Basque twist, featuring tender chicken in a rich, peppery sauce, served with caramelized onions and mushrooms, all made with 00-flour for a low-carb friendly option.
instructions: Season the chicken thighs with salt and pepper, then coat with 00-flour, shaking off excess.
Heat butter in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat, then add chicken and cook until browned, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove chicken from pot, then add sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until caramelized, about 20 minutes.
Add sliced mushrooms to pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add peppercorns, red wine, and chicken broth to pot, stirring to scrape up browned bits from bottom.
Return chicken to pot, then add thyme sprig and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover pot, and simmer, stirring occasionally, until chicken is cooked through, about 25 minutes.
Remove pot from heat, then let cool slightly before serving.
Serve chicken and sauce over a bed of sauteed spinach or roasted vegetables, garnished with fresh thyme.
